observation.images.agentview_image.comment: Scene view (robosuite agentview). uint8 HWC from sim bridge; agent view first by camera-order convention. OPEN: LIBERO-PRO perturbs textures/lighting/camera at eval, so the pixel distribution can drift outside standard-LIBERO ranges (the [0,255] dtype bound still holds).

observation.text.comment: Task instruction. LIBERO-PRO additionally applies instruction perturbations (paraphrase/corruption) at eval; the channel and dtype are unchanged vs standard LIBERO.

observation.state.robot0_eef_pos.comment: Absolute eef position in the robot base frame.

observation.state.robot0_eef_axis_angle.comment: Absolute eef orientation as axis-angle, base frame (converted from robosuite's xyzw quaternion).

action.delta_eef_pos.comment: OSC_POSE translation delta, base frame. kp/kd = robosuite OSC default (critically damped). min/max = clip bounds.

action.delta_eef_axis_angle.comment: OSC_POSE rotation delta vs current eef (frame=eef).

comment: Realtime (free-running) bridge for LIBERO-PRO -- the realtime analogue of envs/libero_pro.json, exactly as libero_realtime is to libero. The robot, cameras, eef-pose+gripper state and OSC EE-delta action are IDENTICAL to envs/libero_pro.json (same robot_type, same per-quantity 'order' split, same stats/limits); the embodiment and the perturbed LIBERO-PRO eval distribution (object/position/instruction/environment perturbations at rollout) are unchanged. The ONLY addition is the realtime mechanism: unlike the synchronous env, the sim does NOT freeze waiting for an action -- it advances on its own control clock (control_hz). An env-side ActionProvider owns the action queue: every control tick it pops the next action (or a safe HOLD no-op on underrun) and steps the sim, while the agent streams whole action chunks asynchronously. The launch-time realtime decision variables are advertised here in the top-level 'inference' block -- inference_mode (one of sync, sync_freeze, naive_async, weighted_async, rtc), threshold (re-infer when queue_remaining <= threshold; the blocking sync/sync_freeze modes force threshold 0), execution_horizon (RTC min execution horizon), control_hz (realtime control-clock rate) -- so the agent pairs its inference behavior (e.g. real-time chunking / RTC) to the env purely from the contract. Defaults shown (rtc, 12, 25, 10 Hz) mirror libero_realtime's DEFAULT_PARAMS. OPEN: stats are the standard-LIBERO distribution, not a bound (perturbations + realtime timing jitter can exceed them); OPEN: the realtime LIBERO-PRO sim bridge is not yet implemented -- this contract specs the intended pairing by analogy to envs/libero_realtime.
